info.title: Platform - Access Management
info.description: The Access Management APIs enable you to programmatically control user access, manage organizational hierarchies, and monitor authentication activities across your OneTrust platform.

hostname.description: The OneTrust hostname such as app.onetrust.com, app-eu.onetrust.com, app-de.onetrust.com, app-uk.onetrust.com, app-apac.onetrust.com, trial.onetrust.com, or uat.onetrust.com.

tags.name: Audit Records
tags.description: APIs to manage and retrieve audit logs for user activities, including login history and access patterns. These endpoints help you monitor security events, track user authentication attempts, and maintain compliance with audit requirements.

tags.name: OAuth Token
tags.description: APIs to manage OAuth 2.0 authentication for secure API access. Generate access tokens using client credentials, retrieve token information, and manage API authentication for your applications.

tags.name: Organizations
tags.description: APIs to manage your organizational hierarchy and structure. Create, update, and delete organizations, define parent-child relationships, and configure organization-specific settings such as default languages and approvers.

tags.name: User Groups
tags.description: APIs to manage user groups and their memberships. Create groups to organize users, assign permissions collectively, add or remove members, and retrieve information about existing groups and their members.

tags.name: User Groups V2
tags.description: APIs to manage user groups and their memberships. Create groups to organize users, assign permissions collectively, add or remove members, and retrieve information about existing groups and their members.

tags.name: Users V2
tags.description: APIs to manage users and their memberships and access levels.

get.description: Use this API to retrieve a list of all organizations within the organizational hierarchy.

post.description:
Use this API to create an organization within the organizational hierarchy.

> 🗒 Things to Know
>
> - The organization will be created as a child of the organization specified in the `parentExternalId` parameter. If a `parentExternalId`value is not specified in the request, the organization will be created as a child of the root organization.

get.summary: Get Audit Records for Login History
get.description:
Use this API to retrieve the login history of all users or a specific user for audit purposes.

> 🗒 Things to Know
>
> - If a date range and specific `userId` is not specified in the request, the response will include the login history for all users over the past 7 days.
> - If a date range is not specified but a specific `userId` is specified in the request, the response will include the login history for that specific user over the past 365 days.
> - If a date range is specified but a specific `userId` is not specified in the request, the response will include the login history for all users over the specified date range.
> - If the date range does not fall within the last 365 days, the response will return an error.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of a specific user to filter login history.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description: The start date for the login history search. Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description: The end date for the login history search. Format: YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number to retrieve.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The number of records per page.

post.description:
Use this API to generate an OAuth access token using the `client_credentials` grant type.

> 🗒 Things to Know
>
> - Client credentials can be generated from within Global Settings in the OneTrust application. For more information, see [Managing OAuth 2.0 Client Credentials](/onetrust/docs/managing-oauth-20-client-credentials).
> - Use the `client_id` and `client_secret` to generate the OAuth access token using this API.
> - A maximum of 500 OAuth 2.0 client credentials can be created per account.

401.description:
Unauthorized

### Possible Reasons:
* Invalid client credentials

post.description:
Use this API to add users as members of a specific user group. 

> 🗒 Things to Know
>
> - When a user is added as a member of a user group, the user inherits the role and organization configured for that user group. If the user belongs to other user groups, the user will still retain existing membership with those groups in addition to their other existing roles and permissions.
> - The `userId` of the users to be added to the user group can be specified in the request body in a comma separated list.

parameters.name: userGroupId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user group.

get.description: Use this API to retrieve a list of user groups, with support for pagination and filtering.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The page number for the results to be retrieved.

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: The number of records to be retrieved per page.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: The sorting criteria in the format: `property,(asc|desc)`. The default sort order is ascending. Multiple sort criteria are supported.

parameters.name: filters
parameters.description: The filters to apply to the user groups.

Contains filter.summary: Filter by name containing value 'ministr'. Value should prefixed and suffixed by % characters

Starts with filter.summary: Filter by name starting with 'Admin'. Value should suffixed by % character

Ends with filter.summary: Filter by name ending with 'tors'. Value should prefixed by % character

get.summary: Get Audit Records for User's Profile
get.description: Use this API to retrieve a list of changes made to a user's profile for audit purposes. The response will include a history of changes made to the user's details, roles, and organizations.

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description:
>-
 `userId` can be retireved using the [Retrieve list of
 Users](https://{$$.env.developer}/api-reference/general/user-provisioning/user-provisioning-apis/users/getallusersusingget)
 API

parameters.name: includeChildObjects
parameters.description: Whether to include child object activities

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Results page to be retrieved (0..N). Example- '?page=1

parameters.name: size
parameters.description: Number of records per page (1…N).

429.description:
Too Many Requests.

organizationId.description: The unique identifier of the organization.

roleIds.description: The list of role identifiers to be associated with the organization.

defaultOrganization.description: The indicator of whether the provided organization will be treated as the default organization.

firstName.description: The first name of the user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user.

internal.description: The indicator of whether the user is an internal user.

expirationDateTime.description: User expiration date in the system

externalId.description: The external identifier for the user.

id.description: The internal identifier of the user group.

name.description: The name of the user group.

description.description: The description of the user group.
description.example: User group used for grouping users

createdBy.description: The unique identifier of the user who created the user group.

createdDate.description: The timestamp of when the user group was created.

lastModifiedBy.description: The unique identifier of the user who last modified the user group.

lastModifiedDate.description: The timestamp of when the user group was last modified.

name.description: The name of the user group.

description.description: The description of the user group.
description.example: User group used for grouping users

currentOrganizationId.description: The unique identifier of the organization currently associated with the user group.

currentRoleId.description: The unique identifier of the role currently associated with the user group.

newOrganizationId.description: The unique identifier of the replacement organization.

newRoleId.description: The unique identifier of the replacement role.

name.description: The name of the user group.

description.description: A brief description of the user group.

userGroupId.description: The unique identifier of the user group.

name.description: The name of the user group.

description.description: The brief description of the user group.

createdBy.description: The unique identifier of the user who created this record.

createdDate.description: The date and time the record was created.

lastModifiedBy.description: The unique identifier of the user who last modified the record.

lastModifiedDate.description: The date and time the record was last modified.

externalId.description: The external identifier of the organization. Accepts alphanumeric characters, underscores, periods, colons, and hyphens.
